claims.description: Verify-first re-confirmation of the 8 gaps (G1-G8) grounded at current main; assertCanReadProfile primitive (self OR guardian-of-uncredentialed-charge, mirrors verifyPersonOwnershipV2/assertCallerIsAccountOwner) applied to every confirmed READ gap: G1 recaps/self, G2 learner-profile, G3 learner-profile/export-text, G4 notes x7, G5 consent/my-status, G7 progress x15, G8 sessions x5 + quiz x3. G6 (consent write-branch) is out of scope per PM ruling.
G2/G3 learner-profile + export-text guard call sites
G4 notes guard call sites (7 GET handlers)
G7 progress guard call sites (15 GET handlers)
G8 sessions guard call sites (5 GET handlers incl. G8d transcript)
G8 quiz guard call sites (3 GET handlers)

Break test per HIGH cluster: authenticated as owner A, X-Profile-Id spoofed to credentialed peer B (same org, no authority relationship) -> 403 and response body contains none of B's seeded data. Representative route per cluster: G1 recaps/self, G2 learner-profile, G3 export-text, G4 notes book route.

Guardian/self allowed negative control: caller reading own profile (self) returns 200 with own data; guardian of an uncredentialed charge reading X-Profile-Id=charge returns 200 with the charge's data (recaps + learner-profile); non-owner peer self-read is not owner-gated.

Red-green-revert on GET /recaps/self (representative HIGH route): fails at current main (peer's recap leaks, 200 instead of 403), passes after the fix (403), fails again on revert (200), restored to green. Executed cycle recorded in wi2416-rgr-evidence.md.

Forward-only TS-AST route-scanner ratchet: walks apps/api/src/routes/*.ts for GET handlers consuming the header-resolved profileId (withProfile/requireProfileId/bare c.get('profileId')) without a assertCanReadProfile call; every pre-existing gap outside G1-G8 scope is grandfathered in profile-read-authority-baseline.json (73 entries); demonstrated red on a synthetic unguarded route and on a live regression (temporarily removing the G1 guard), then reverted to green.
